key_observations: Strong volume progression: jumped from 6-10km/week (May-Jun) to 15-19km/week (late Jun-Jul), representing 150%+ increase in training load over 3 weeks
Heart rate elevation suggests intensifying efforts—avg HR climbed from 158→160→164 bpm across weeks, with July 6 spike to 175.7 bpm avg indicating high-intensity work
Pace consistency improving despite higher HR: maintaining 5:47-6:12/km pace while running harder (higher avg HR), suggesting aerobic fitness gains
warnings: Rapid load increase (23.6km gap week-to-week Jun 22→29) carries injury risk; ensure adequate recovery days and listen to injury signals
Moderate fatigue signals present: elevated resting/average HR trend + only 2 runs in final week suggests possible deload/recovery week needed soon
recommendations: Implement 1-2 true easy/recovery runs at 140-150 bpm next week to consolidate gains and manage fatigue before next intensity block
Add 1 structured tempo or interval session weekly (currently missing) to capitalize on aerobic base and direct fitness gains
Monitor weekly mileage: cap increases at 10% per week maximum going forward; you've done well but are at injury threshold with current progression

weeks_analysis.assessment: Single run baseline. Moderate pace (6:11/km), low-moderate volume. No HR data available—equipment/connectivity issue?
Slight volume dip, consistent pace (6:12/km). Suggests recovery week or reduced commitment period. Still single-run weeks.
Lowest volume in dataset (6.3km). Single run, improved pace (5:47/km) despite low frequency—possible speedwork or fresh legs.
Critical pivot point: 3 runs introduced, volume 2.5x jump. HR data emerges at 158 bpm (moderate intensity). Building phase begins.
Peak volume week. 3 runs maintained, +3.6km from previous week. HR stable at 160 (slight rise). Includes football—cross-training element detected.
Planned reduction to 2 runs (recovery week) but intensity elevated: HR +4 bpm, peak HR hit 192 bpm on Jul 6. Deload in volume, not intensity.
